highlights.text: I wrote on Thursday at 4pm: “My general lean is that ES can hold 6592 though (or quick trap below).
I wrote yesterday at 4pm: “ My general lean is this can keep going.
One of those building blocks is the elevator down sell and this refers to a period of non-stop, rapid, headline driven selling.
Every point of elevator down red WILL get paid back at least 50% but often considerably more in the form of a vicious squeeze.
The catch though, is these only occur when my core setup - the Failed Breakdown - triggers.
We saw it last Monday March as stated: ES plunged to 6353 last Monday evening going elevator down .
An alternative is to wait for it to flush and recover (perhaps to 6353) for a Failed Breakdown.” We got this trigger at 830pm last Monday evening, and were off to 6647 last weeks 6650 high week.

Target slate is 6662, 6677, 6702, 6716+.” Overnight, we hit 6662 almost exact, then spent this morning trapping and putting in Failed Breakdowns of 6592 again, offering more entries for anyone who missed the Sunday entry.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow

post_title: SPX Is Coiled For A Trend Move. What Way? April 8th Plan
